input.initialPrompt:
I have an ObservableCollection<Product> with Name and Price properties.
Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using
CollectionView with a DataTemplate that shows both the name and price.
Use compiled bindings.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**Product.cs**
```csharp
public class Product
{
    public string Name { get; set; }
    public decimal Price { get; set; }
}
```

**MainPage.xaml**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:local="clr-namespace:YourApp"
             x:Class="YourApp.MainPage"
             x:DataType="local:MainPageViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Products}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="local:Product">
                <HorizontalStackLayout Padding="10" Spacing="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                           FontSize="16"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}"
                           FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </HorizontalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

**MainPageViewModel.cs**
```csharp
using System.Collections.ObjectModel;

public class MainPageViewModel
{
    public ObservableCollection<Product> Products { get; } = new()
    {
        new Product { Name = "Widget", Price = 9.99m },
        new Product { Name = "Gadget", Price = 24.95m },
    };
}
```

**Key points:**
- **`x:DataType`** on the `ContentPage` enables compiled bindings for the page-level context; the **`x:DataType`** on the `DataTemplate` does the same for each item — this gives compile-time checking and better performance.
- `CollectionView` scrolls vertically by default, so no extra configuration is needed.
- `ObservableCollection` ensures the UI updates automatically when items are added/removed.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) code example showing how to use CollectionView with compiled bindings to display a list of products. This is a straightforward code example request - no need to search the codebase.
